data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G

project.title: Multiplexed isobaric quantitative Protein Interaction Reporter (iqPIR) cross-linking on standard proteins
project.projectDescription: Initial demonstration of multiplexed isobaric quantitative Protein Interaction Reporter technology using two multiplexed samples consisting of 5 cross-linked standard proteins including alcohol dehydrogenase 1 (ADH1_YEAST), albumin (ALBU_BOVIN), cytochrome c (CYC_BOVIN), hemoglobin (HBA_HUMAN), and myoglobin (MYG_HORSE).
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Proteins were dissolved separately at 1 mg/mL into a buffered solution of 170 mM Na2HPO4, pH 8.  A final concentration of 1 mM of each iqPIR (2 μl from 50 mM stock in DMSO) was added to 100 μL aliquots of each protein, corresponding to 100 μg.  The reaction was allowed to proceed for 30 min at room temperature with constant mixing.  Disulfide bonds in the proteins were reduced with 5 mM tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP) for 30 min at room tem-perature followed by alkylation of thiols with 10 mM iodo-acetamide (IAA) for 30 min at room temperature.  Proteins were digested by adding a 1:200 ratio of trypsin to protein and incubating the samples at 37 oC overnight with constant mixing.  Resulting peptide samples were acidified to 1% (v/v) TFA prior to desalting with 1cc (50 mg) sized C18 SepPak cartridges (Waters, Milford, MA) loaded onto a vacuum manifold.  After equilibrating the SepPak with 1 mL of ACN, 0.1% TFA followed by 3 mL of H2O, 0.1% TFA the acidified samples were loaded onto the SepPak cartridges.  Salt was removed by washing bound peptides with 3 mL H2O, 0.1% TFA.  Peptides were eluted into 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes with 1 mL of 80% ACN, 0.1% TFA.  Desalted samples were concentrated to ~100 μL by vacu-um centrifugation.  Samples were adjusted to a peptide concentration of 0.3 μg/μL with H20, 0.1% FA prior to LC-MS analysis on a Easy-nLC coupled with a Q-Exactive Plus mass spectometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw files were converted to mzXML format.  Mango was used to identify MS2 spectra containing a PIR mass relationship indicating a cross-linked peptide pair.  MS2 spectra containing PIR mass relationships were searched using Comet, followed by analsyis with PeptideProphet, iProphet and validation with XLinkProphet.  Results were filtered to an estimated false discovery rate of less than 1%.  Quantification of iqPIR signal was performed with custom developed software.  Resulting data was uploaded into XLinkDB
